key_departures.impact: The Cardinals released Murray at the start of the new league year, ending a 7-year era. Murray struggled in 2025 with injuries and inconsistent play, ultimately being replaced by Jacoby Brissett. Arizona absorbs a massive $54.7M dead cap hit. Brissett remains under contract as the presumed starter.

Thompson signed a 3-year, $36M max deal with the Cowboys. He had 95 tackles, 6 PDs and 15 starts in 2025. Was a key starter in Arizona's secondary for 7 seasons. Dadrion Taylor-Demerson is expected to step into a starting role alongside Budda Baker, but the loss is significant.

Campbell (PFF 72.2 grade in 2025) is a UFA and has not re-signed. A franchise legend who still contributed as a pass rusher. His departure creates a gap in the IDL rotation, though Arizona has Darius Robinson, Walter Nolen, and Dante Stills under contract.

Williams appeared in only 15 of 34 possible games across two seasons due to injuries and enters free agency. With Kelvin Beachum also a free agent, Arizona has a clear hole at right tackle with only Paris Johnson Jr. locked in on the left side.

Beachum is a free agent. As a veteran depth/swing tackle he provided value but was not a starting solution at right tackle. His departure is lower-impact given his backup role, but leaves the team thinner behind Paris Johnson Jr.

Cardinals released Tomlinson on March 6, saving ~$9.4M in cap space. He accounted for 555 defensive snaps in 2025. A meaningful IDL body is gone, which the team has attempted to address by signing Roy Lopez and re-signing LJ Collier.
Released March 5 alongside Akeem Davis-Gaither, saving $10M+ in cap space. Nichols (PFF 35.5 grade) was a below-average rotational IDL piece. Low-impact loss.
Released March 5. A below-average linebacker (PFF 49.9) who was part of a thin LB room. His release modestly opens up depth opportunities for younger players but LB was already a need.
UFA. Was Arizona's slot receiver and special teams contributor. The Cardinals did not re-sign him. Their slot receiver depth is thin after also losing Zay Jones.
UFA. Jones had limited production and was not expected to return. His departure is mostly a slot/depth loss, now partially addressed with Bourne's signing.

signings.analysis: Pro Bowl guard (2024) who started 44 consecutive games for the Steelers with a 73.3 PFF grade in 2025. Three-year deal at $10.5M AAV is comfortably within the starter tier for IOL. He slides into the RG spot and addresses one of Arizona's two IOL starting vacancies. Evan Brown holds LG, so Seumalo completes the starting guard pair. IOL need is partially resolved — still may want a center upgrade via draft, but the guard need is done.

One-year deal signals a bridge/backup at best. Minshew is expected to back up Jacoby Brissett, who is Arizona's presumed starter after Murray's release. The Cardinals missed out on Malik Willis (went to Dolphins on 3yr/$67.5M) and are now operating with Brissett and Minshew as their QB room — neither is a long-term answer. This is a depth move, not a solution. QB remains a top draft priority.

Allgeier gives Arizona a physical short-yardage back to complement James Conner (restructured) and Trey Benson. Arizona finished 31st in rushing in 2025 due to injuries to both Conner and Benson. At $6M AAV on 2 years, this is solidly mid-tier RB money. Allgeier is a complementary piece, not a bell cow, but the RB room now has legitimate depth. RB need is partially addressed — depth is solid but a true lead back is still needed if Conner or Benson can't stay healthy.

Lopez returns to Arizona (he played there in 2023-24 before one year in Detroit) on a 2yr/$11.5M deal with $7M guaranteed — meaningful money for an IDL piece. He played 425 snaps for the Lions, recorded 2 sacks, and graded 33rd among 127 IDL on PFF. He fills the void left by Tomlinson's release and should start alongside Darius Robinson. IDL need is partially addressed — the Tomlinson/Nichols holes are patched, but adding a true disruptive pass rusher in the interior would still help.

Bourne (37 receptions, 551 yards for SF in 2025) joins Marvin Harrison Jr. and Michael Wilson. Two-year deal at $5M AAV with a max of $12M is depth/rotational money for WR. He had a prior relationship with new HC Mike LaFleur from their SF days together. The Cardinals' slot depth issue (post-Dortch/Jones) is marginally addressed, but Bourne has never topped 37 catches in his last four seasons. WR depth is slightly improved but the room isn't meaningfully upgraded for 2026 — Harrison and Wilson remain the core.

Fourth consecutive one-year deal with Arizona. Collier had a knee injury in Week 2 of 2025 and played only 4 games. When healthy in 2024, he was productive (3.5 sacks in 17 games). This is a depth/rotational re-sign with continuity value. One-year minimum-adjacent deal signals low commitment. IDL need unchanged.

Conner restructured his existing deal to remain in Arizona. He's 31 and suffered a gruesome foot injury in Week 3 of 2025. On restructured terms at ~$9.8M cap hit, he's retained as the nominal lead back but is a major injury risk at his age. Combined with Allgeier and Benson, the room has depth but volatility at the top. RB need partially addressed.

Murphy-Bunting agreed to a revised deal to stay with Arizona. Contract terms not yet disclosed. He was an expected cap casualty but restructured to remain. Arizona has Will Johnson, Garrett Williams, Denzel Burke, and Max Melton in the CB room. Murphy-Bunting's return adds veteran depth but with a revised (lower) deal, this is a depth/rotational role. CB room has young talent and this keeps a veteran in the mix.
One-year deal to bring back the kicker. Was not a pressing need. Special teams continuity maintained.
One-year deal to bring back the punter. Missed most of 2025 with a back injury but is now healthy. Special teams continuity maintained.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Murray's release is now official, and the Cardinals missed on Malik Willis (signed with Dolphins). The room is Jacoby Brissett + Gardner Minshew (1yr/$8.25M backup deal). Neither is a long-term answer. QB is now the most critical need heading into the draft — Arizona holds the #3 overall pick. This need escalated dramatically.

Both Jonah Williams (injury-plagued, 15 starts in 34 games) and Kelvin Beachum (37, UFA) have departed. Paris Johnson Jr. is locked in at LT but right tackle is completely unaddressed in free agency. No OT signing has been made. OT remains a top-tier need, possibly top-2 with QB.

Isaac Seumalo's 3yr/$31.5M deal fills the RG vacancy with a Pro Bowl starter. Evan Brown holds LG. The guard spots are now settled. Center may still be worth addressing in mid-rounds of the draft, but the most urgent IOL need is resolved. Drops from priority need to moderate depth consideration.

Jalen Thompson departed to Dallas (3yr/$36M). Dadrion Taylor-Demerson is expected to step into a starting role alongside Budda Baker, and Kitan Crawford impressed in 2025. The position is not a crisis, but losing a proven 7-year starter creates a genuine upgrade need. Bump one tier — draft or late FA target warranted.

Arizona lost Tomlinson (released) and Nichols (released) and did not re-sign Campbell. However, they signed Roy Lopez (2yr/$11.5M, $7M gtd) back and re-signed LJ Collier ($2.5M). Lopez is a legitimate starter who filled a starting role in Detroit. Combined with Darius Robinson, Walter Nolen, and Dante Stills under contract, the IDL room has bodies. Still could use a premium pass rusher inside, but the depth hole is patched. Drops one tier.

Dortch and Jones departed; Kendrick Bourne signed (2yr/$10M base). Bourne is a depth piece, not a starter upgrade. The top of the room (Harrison Jr. + Wilson) is solid. Bourne addresses slot depth at low AAV. Need is technically the same tier — depth is okay but Arizona still wouldn't turn down a WR with upside in rounds 3-5.

No EDGE signing was made. Josh Sweat (12 sacks in 2025) is the only proven pass rusher on the roster. Arizona produced just 30 total sacks in 2025, third-fewest in the NFL. Sweat needs a running mate badly. No free agent EDGE was added. This is still a clear draft priority.

Davis-Gaither was released but was below-average anyway (PFF 49.9). Cody Simon and Mack Wilson project as the starting LB duo. No LB free agent signed. Need unchanged — depth behind the starters remains thin but it's not a top-tier hole.

James Conner restructured to stay, Tyler Allgeier signed (2yr/$12M), and Trey Benson remains under contract. The backfield now has three bodies with different skill profiles. Depth and redundancy are addressed. RB is no longer a meaningful draft need — a luxury pick at most.

key_additions.headline: Pro Bowl guard fills urgent RG vacancy, anchors rebuilt interior OL alongside Evan Brown
Familiar face returns to replace Tomlinson; projects as starting DT with $7M guaranteed signaling real investment
Physical short-yardage back adds depth to injury-depleted backfield that ranked 31st in rushing in 2025
Bridge backup joins Brissett after Cardinals miss on Malik Willis; QB room needs major draft upgrade
NFC West departure reunites Bourne with HC Mike LaFleur; depth piece for a receiver room led by Harrison Jr. and Wilson

key_losses.headline: Seven-year era ends — Murray released with massive dead cap hit, leaving Cardinals without a franchise QB heading into the draft
7-year Cardinals starter (95 tackles, 15 starts in 2025) departs, opening a hole next to Budda Baker
555-snap interior lineman cut in cap purge, creating DT void now patched by Lopez and Collier
Injury-plagued RT (15 starts in 34 games over 2 years) departs, leaving right tackle an unaddressed need entering the draft
Franchise legend (PFF 72.2 in 2025 at age 39) hits free agency without a reported deal; IDL pass-rush presence lost
